metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Investigation of Medicinal Products for the Treatment of Urinary Incontinence

rules.rule_text: Separate studies are mandatory for investigation of UI in patients with neurogenic incontinence, incontinence associated with radiotherapy, brachytherapy, cryosurgery, HIFU, radical cystectomy, or neobladder.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is mandatory that separate studies are conducted for the investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of UI in patients with these conditions.
rules.exceptions: Study design principles and outcome measures from this guideline may still be applied

rules.rule_text: Diagnostic criteria for clinical studies during drug development may need to be tighter than those in normal clinical practice. A definitive diagnosis cannot usually be made based on symptoms only, particularly for Phase II proof-of-concept studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The diagnostic criteria that can be considered sufficient for the purpose of clinical studies during drug development may have to be tighter than those applicable in normal clinical practice. A definitive diagnosis cannot usually be made based on symptoms only. This is particularly relevant for phase II studies where proof of concept for the investigational product should be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: Urodynamic studies are recommended in addition to history, clinical examination, and micturition diaries to confirm diagnosis of stress, urge, or mixed incontinence for registration studies where possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: They are hence recommended in addition to history and clinical examination and to micturition diaries to confirm the diagnosis of stress, urge or mixed incontinence for the purpose of registration studies where possible.
rules.exceptions: OAB cannot be defined at condition level by urodynamics

rules.rule_text: Urodynamic data must be interpreted by at least 2, preferably 3, qualified independent reviewers with consensus. ICS standardization of test procedures must be employed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Tests should be interpreted and consensus should be reached by at least 2, preferably 3, qualified, independent reviewers. It is highly important that standardization of test procedures as recommended by the ICS is employed when performing and interpreting urodynamic studies.

rules.rule_text: Phase II studies require parallel group design with a placebo arm. Minimum study duration is 6 weeks for new substance classes; minimum 4 weeks for classes with well-established time to maximal effect. Several dose levels must be studied.
rules.verbatim_quote: Parallel group design including one placebo arm is recommended... a study duration of six weeks is the minimum acceptable time for new classes of substances. For substance classes with well-established time to maximal effect, a shorter study period of no less than four weeks may be acceptable if adequately justified. Several dose levels should be studied to establish the optimal dose.

rules.rule_text: In Phase II, the highest level of diagnosis ('condition' level with urodynamic confirmation) is generally recommended. Men and women should preferably be investigated/analysed separately due to difficulty separating prostate-related symptoms from incontinence.
rules.verbatim_quote: The highest level of diagnosis ("condition", see section 5.2) is generally recommended in phase II studies. As it is difficult to separate prostate-related symptoms in men from incontinence not related to obstruction, it is preferred that men and women are investigated / analysed separately.
rules.exceptions: If separate studies are not performed, stratification by gender is required and both subgroups must be analysed separately

rules.rule_text: The primary endpoint in Phase II should be a urodynamic parameter appropriate for the intended condition. For OAB (not defined at condition level), an appropriate clinical endpoint should be used instead.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that the primary endpoint in therapeutic exploratory studies (phase II) is a urodynamic parameter which is appropriate for the intended condition to be studied... as it is essential that proof of concept is demonstrated. An exception is OAB, which is not defined at condition level. Here, an appropriate clinical endpoint (see section 6.3.4) should be used.
rules.exceptions: OAB studies use clinical endpoints instead

rules.rule_text: Phase III trials should primarily include patients with 'pure' stress or urge incontinence. Mixed incontinence patients may be included but the type studied must be the major complaint. Disease severity must be clearly defined using validated grading systems.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to primarily include patients with "pure" stress or urge incontinence in studies of stress or urge incontinence... It is important that the type of incontinence to be studied is the major complaint of the patient. Disease severity should be clearly defined using validated grading systems.
rules.exceptions: Patients with mixed incontinence may be included for practical reasons

rules.rule_text: OAB associated with BPH in men must be investigated in a separate study. BPH must be optimally treated pharmacologically or surgically, and BPH treatment should remain unchanged during the study period.
rules.verbatim_quote: OAB in men is not infrequently associated with benign prostate hyperplasia. Such patients must be investigated in a separate study. BPH must be optimally treated pharmacologically (with alpha-1 adrenoreceptor antagonists alone or in combination with a 5-alpha reductase inhibitor) or surgically. Treatment for BPH should remain unchanged during the study period whenever possible.

rules.rule_text: Older patients (65-74, 75-84, and >=85 years) must be included in sufficient numbers in Phase III studies. Randomization must be stratified by age group.
rules.verbatim_quote: Older patients, specified as 65 - 74, 75 - 84, and >=85 years of age, should be included in phase III studies in sufficient numbers to permit evaluation of efficacy and safety in the older population. The randomization should be stratified by age group.

rules.rule_text: All Phase III studies must be double-blind. If double-blind is impossible, physicians assessing clinical outcomes must remain unaware of treatment assignments.
rules.verbatim_quote: Randomization and concealment of allocation is considered essential. All studies should be double-blind unless this design is considered to be impossible... If a double-blind study is not feasible, every effort must be made to ensure that the physicians who assess clinical outcomes remain unaware of treatment assignments.

rules.rule_text: Stress incontinence studies must be placebo-controlled. Comparison with approved drug therapies is not mandatory but a 3-arm study (investigational product, placebo, active comparator) is a possible approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies of stress incontinence should be placebo controlled. Comparisons with non-drug and non-surgical therapies (e.g. pelvic floor exercise, vaginal devices) are encouraged but may pose problems with study design. Comparison with approved drug therapies for stress incontinence could provide additional information but is not considered mandatory.

rules.rule_text: Urge incontinence drugs must be tested in placebo-controlled trials because available drugs do not consistently show superiority over placebo. A comparator arm with best available treatment may be added.
rules.verbatim_quote: Drugs intended for the use in urge incontinence should be tested in placebo controlled trials as available drugs for urge incontinence do not consistently show superiority over placebo. A comparator arm including the best available treatment may be added to allow demonstrating superior efficacy or safety.

rules.rule_text: Patient perception of treatment effect must be included in the primary endpoint for Phase III trials. Changes in quantitative symptom measures alone cannot serve as a sole endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: patient perception of treatment effect should be included in the primary endpoint in phase III trials. Changes in quantitative symptom measures allow a quantification of symptoms but cannot serve as a sole endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Two design strategies for primary endpoints are acceptable: (1) a single objective endpoint with mandatory responder analysis using clinically relevant MICD, or (2) two co-primary endpoints where one is objective and the second is patient-perceived effect (e.g., QoL). Composite endpoints are not recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: 1. A single, "objective" endpoint (for example number of incontinence episodes) which is analysed using standard statistical techniques. To further assess clinical relevance, a responder analysis should be performed. 2. A design with two co-primary endpoints... one of the endpoints should be an "objective" (e.g. number of incontinence episodes) one, and the second endpoint strongly related to the patient perceived effect (for example QoL). A composite endpoint is not recommended.

rules.rule_text: For stress incontinence, the primary objective endpoints are incidence of incontinence episodes and/or amount of urine leaked (micturition diary, pad weighing test). For urge incontinence, co-primary endpoints are incidence of urgency, frequency of incontinence episodes, amount of urine leaked, and/or frequency of micturition.
rules.verbatim_quote: For stress incontinence the incidence of incontinence episodes and/or amount of urine leaked may be used (e. g. micturition diary, pad weighing test). For urge incontinence the incidence of urgency, the frequency of incontinence episodes, and/or amount of urine leaked (e.g. pad weighing test) and/or the frequency of micturition are recommended as co-primary endpoints.

rules.rule_text: HRQL data can never be the sole basis for efficacy claims in urinary incontinence. HRQL instruments must be properly validated in the target population, and clinically relevant change in pre-specified QoL domains must be defined in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: HRQL data should be considered an extension of an evaluation of efficacy, which can provide meaningful information to the prescriber and the patient. HRQL data can, however, never be the sole basis for efficacy claims.

rules.rule_text: A micturition diary recording events (micturition, leakage, urgency, pad use) must be kept for at least 3 days. A diary measuring volumes and pad weight increase must be kept for 48-72 hours, never less than 24 hours.
rules.verbatim_quote: A diary including only recording of events (micturition, leakage, urgency and pad use) should and could usually be kept for at least 3 days. A diary including measuring of volumes and pad weight increase should be kept for as long a period as possible, usually for 48-72 hours but never less than 24 hours.

rules.rule_text: Minimum Phase III study duration is at least 3 months for efficacy evaluation. Total study duration must be at least 12 months for adequate safety database, which may include an open-label extension with appropriate justification.
rules.source_section: 6.3.5 Timing of assessments and study duration
rules.verbatim_quote: To allow appropriate evaluation of efficacy of an investigational drug, a study duration of at least 3 months is expected... a further follow up is necessary so that the total study duration is at least 12 months; this may be performed as an open label design if appropriate justification can be provided.
rules.exceptions: Open-label extension acceptable for the 12-month safety follow-up

rules.rule_text: Safety data covering at least 12 months is required. Special attention must be given to anticholinergic, sedative, and orthostatic effects and effects on locomotor system in older patients. Monitoring for increased residual urine and urinary tract infections is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: For urinary incontinence as a chronic disorder, safety data covering at least a 12 month period are required and long-term safety of new therapeutic interventions has to be established... Especially anticholinergic, sedative, and orthostatic effects and effects on the locomotor system should be studied in older patients.

rules.rule_text: Prostate cancer monitoring must be considered if hormonally active substances are investigated for urinary incontinence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prostate cancer monitoring should be considered if hormonally active substances are investigated.

rules.rule_text: The primary analysis population must be the ITT population. A secondary analysis counting indeterminate or missing outcomes as failures must also be performed. Compliance and reasons for non-compliance must be documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary analysis population should be the ITT population. In all studies there should at least be a comparison between the primary analysis and an analysis of all randomized patients in which indeterminate or missing outcomes are counted as failures as it is expected that a relevant number of patients will drop out of the studies due to lack of efficacy.

rules.rule_text: A clinically relevant cut-off (minimal important clinical difference, MICD) for the proposed primary endpoint must be defined for each clinical study, justified using scientific data from sources other than the study itself.
rules.verbatim_quote: A clinically relevant cut-off (minimal clinical relevant difference) for the proposed primary endpoint has to be defined for each clinical study. A responder analysis should be performed as a secondary analysis if a single "objective" endpoint is used.

rules.rule_text: Superiority versus placebo in well-conducted randomised studies is recommended. Absence of a placebo arm even in actively controlled trials requires very sound justification and advance discussion with regulatory authority.
rules.verbatim_quote: Superiority versus placebo in well-conducted randomized studies is recommended... The absence of a placebo control arm even in actively controlled trials in UI would require very sound justification and should be discussed with the regulatory authority in advance.

rules.rule_text: Non-inferiority trial designs are discouraged for regulatory purposes in urinary incontinence until treatments are available for at least one form of UI, because adequate evidence of assay sensitivity is lacking.
rules.verbatim_quote: Until treatments are available for at least one of the forms of urinary incontinence where that is the case, non-inferiority studies for regulatory purposes are discouraged. Even if there was an appropriately licensed treatment, the issue of assay sensitivity would still need to be fully addressed.
rules.exceptions: Exception where it is ethically impossible to enrol patients into a placebo arm, but regulatory advice must be sought

rules.rule_text: For tissue engineered products (TEPs) for stress urinary incontinence, Phase II urodynamic studies must be complemented with structural studies (rhabdosphincter volume, thickening, contractility, tissue integration). Phase II study duration must be at least 6 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the development of a tissue engineered product, the urodynamic studies in phase II (see section 6) should be complemented with structural studies... For the purpose of TEP, a study duration of at least 6 months in phase II is expected to be necessary.
rules.exceptions: Duration may be extended to 12 months depending on type of product

rules.rule_text: TEP Phase III studies for SUI require minimum 1 year study duration with mandatory 2-year follow-up focused on safety. TEPs must demonstrate superiority against nonsurgical standard of care.
rules.verbatim_quote: In therapy development for SUI using TEPs, the minimal study duration of phase III studies should be 1 year with a mandatory follow-up for 2 years, with focus on safety aspects... the superiority against nonsurgical standard of care should be shown.
rules.exceptions: If blinded study not possible, independent evaluation maintaining objectivity must be in place

rules.rule_text: For TEP confirmatory studies, structural endpoints must be considered as co-primary or main secondary endpoints. Only patients with pure stress incontinence should be included.
rules.verbatim_quote: the structural endpoints should be considered as (co)- primary endpoints or as main secondary ones. For the indication SUI, only patients with pure stress incontinence should be included.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric MNE trials, only children with frequent symptoms (>=3 episodes/week over 4 weeks) who have failed reassurance, information, behavioural therapy and enuresis alarms should be included. Minimum age is 6 years per ICCS criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: Diagnosis of MNE should be determined according to International Children's Continence Society (ICCS) criteria and patients should be at least 6 years old at inclusion. Patients with MNE should only be included when reassurance, information, behavioural therapy and enuresis alarms have failed to reduce the symptoms.

rules.rule_text: MNE studies must compare new medicine to placebo. Study duration should be approximately 8-12 weeks. Safety demonstration is critical given MNE is a self-limiting benign condition.
rules.verbatim_quote: A new medicine to be investigated to treat MNE should be compared to placebo. As MNE often resolves, the study medication duration for efficacy and safety should be approximately 8 - 12 weeks... Given that MNE is a benign condition that usually is self-limiting, it is critical that a new medicine is shown to be safe.

rules.rule_text: Idiopathic OAB and neurogenic detrusor overactivity (NDO) in children must be studied in separate trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: Idiopathic OAB and NDO should be studied in separate trials.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric idiopathic OAB patients may only be included in trials after successful treatment of constipation and failure of urotherapy (information and behavioural advice). Minimum age is around 5 years when diagnosis can be reliably established.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with idiopathic OAB should only be included after successful treatment of constipation and when urotherapy, which includes information and behavioural advice, has failed to reduce the symptoms... Children with idiopathic OAB should be included from around the age of 5 years, when a diagnosis can be reliably established.
rules.exceptions: In children <11 years, incontinence should be the inclusion criterion as young children cannot reliably report urgency

rules.rule_text: For paediatric idiopathic OAB, the proposed primary outcome measure is change from baseline in mean volume voided per micturition, documented by patient-reported diary completed by parents for a full week.
rules.source_section: 9.2.3 Study objectives and clinical outcome measures
rules.verbatim_quote: Mean volume voided per micturition (proposed primary outcome measure)... The patient reported outcome should be the primary endpoint and should be documented by use of a diary for recording of events (micturition and incontinence episodes), which should be completed by the parents for a full week.

rules.rule_text: For paediatric NDO, the proposed primary outcome measure is change from baseline in maximum cystometric capacity (MCC) at leakage or at maximal 135% of age-related bladder capacity.
rules.source_section: 9.2.3 Study objectives and clinical outcome measures
rules.verbatim_quote: Maximum cystometric capacity (MCC) at leakage or at maximal 135% of age related bladder capacity (proposed primary outcome measure)

rules.rule_text: Paediatric OAB and NDO studies require 12-month follow-up for long-term safety. After the initial 3-month study, open-label extension is acceptable. Special attention must be paid to effects on urinary retention, growth, and development.
rules.verbatim_quote: As treatment for OAB and NDO may continue for long periods, follow-up in children should continue for 12 months in order to demonstrate long-term safety... Special attention should generally be paid to effects on urinary retention as well as on growth and development.
rules.exceptions: Extension may be open-label after initial 3 months if appropriately justified
